commit.message:
Replace input-constraint asserts with TranslationError in converter (#2845)

Replace 5 assert statements that check input AST constraints with
TranslationError (via self._fail()) to provide meaningful error messages
instead of bare AssertionErrors:

- Compare expressions: require single comparison operator
- Return statements: require a return value
- For loop bounds: require a function call, no keyword arguments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(deploy): add .nojekyll to prevent Jekyll processing on GitHub Pages

GitHub Pages was running Jekyll over the repo root and docs/ markdown files
instead of serving the Vite build artifact.

- Add .nojekyll at repo root (disables Jekyll for branch-based Pages source)
- Add 'touch ./dist/.nojekyll' step in deploy workflow (disables Jekyll
  for GitHub Actions artifact-based deployment)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix mempool-restore.sh silent exit on missing mainnet config

The load_conf_creds function used 'return' without explicit exit code
when the config file didn't exist. With 'set -e', the inherited exit
code 1 from the failed [[ -f ]] test silently aborted the entire script.

Changed to 'return 0' so missing fallback config is treated as success.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add CODEOWNERS for eng/scripts/docs/type2docfx.version.txt (#37570)

Adds `@danieljurek` and `@weshaggard` as CODEOWNERS for
`/eng/scripts/docs/type2docfx.version.txt`.

### Packages impacted by this PR

None — configuration change only.

### Issues associated with this PR

N/A

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

`eng/scripts/docs/type2docfx.version.txt` had no CODEOWNERS entry,
leaving it without designated reviewers for changes to that file.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

Single option: add the entry to `.github/CODEOWNERS` following existing
patterns in that file.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No — CODEOWNERS is a GitHub configuration file with no associated test
infrastructure.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_

N/A

### Checklists
- [ ] Added impacted package name to the issue description.
- [ ] Does this PR need any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so,
create an Issue in the
[Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript)
repository and link it here.)_
- [ ] Added a changelog (if necessary).

commit.message:
fix: 0003 migration を D1 対応に書き直し

D1 では PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF が別ステートメントに持続しないため
DROP TABLE users 時に FK 制約違反が発生していた。

子テーブル(logs, questions)をバックアップ → drop → users 再作成 →
questions/logs 再作成 → データ復元 の順序に変更。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Sprint 60: TextOp shrinking, selection ops, variadic viewport, Cmd monoid laws, layout invariants, LogViewer canonical example

- Fixed TextOpArb shrinker: Arb.fromGen -> Arb.fromGenShrink with shrinkTextOp
- Extended TextOp DU: TSelectLeft, TSelectRight, TSelectWordLeft, TSelectWordRight, TDeleteWordLeft
- Added 12th TextInput property: selection invariants after extend-select operations
- Fixed vacuous 'deleteBackward on empty' test to use variadic ops
- Updated all VirtualList property tests: hardcoded ofArray 5 -> ofArray (max 1 vp) with PositiveInt vp
- Added Cmd monoid laws: left identity, right identity, associativity (property-based + unit tests, 6 total)
- Added layout invariant property tests: Fill fairness, Min constraint, Percentage, n-fill sum (7 tests)
- Added Sample 15 LogViewer: canonical example demonstrating VirtualList+TextInput+FocusRing+Toast+Cmd.copyToClipboard
- Added 21 LogViewer unit tests + 3 snapshot tests (31 sample tests total)
- 2,455 tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

the real OpenTelemetry SDK and OTLP HTTP exporters

assertions, and log emission helpers

retries, and app startup / stream polling

## Testing
- cargo test --quiet
- cargo test --test e2e -- --include-ignored --quiet

commit.message:
feat: Skill queries injected as workflow steps during NL creation

When creating a workflow from natural language, the system now:
1. Generates the base workflow via LLM
2. Finds matching skills by category, tags, and description keywords
3. Injects skill KQL queries as additional executable steps

Example: Creating 'HIPAA Compliance Check' workflow now:
- LLM generates 3 base steps (secure score, recommendations, data posture)
- HIPAA skill matches by tag 'HIPAA' + category 'compliance'
- Skill's 2 KQL queries added as steps: 'Check for PHI Exposure'
  and 'Unencrypted Data Transfer'
- Final workflow has 5 steps, all executable

Response now includes skillsApplied[] and stepsInjected count.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  and CLAUDE.md symlink strategy. Includes 3 reference docs (discovery spec,
  cross-agent compat, 8 project-type templates).

with both canonical names.

commit.message:
Fix QueueProcessor silently dropping messages when function targets a poison queue (#56760)

* Initial plan

* Fix QueueProcessor silently dropping messages when targeting a poison queue

Guard the dequeue count check in BeginProcessingMessageAsync with a
_poisonQueue != null check. When no poison queue is configured (e.g.,
the function targets a poison queue itself), messages should always be
processed regardless of their dequeue count.

Add unit test BeginProcessingMessageAsync_MaxDequeueCountExceeded_NoPoisonQueue_ContinuesProcessing
to verify the fix.

Co-authored-by: mathewc <1056548+mathewc@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update CHANGELOG.md with bug fix note for poison queue MaxDequeueCount issue

Co-authored-by: mathewc <1056548+mathewc@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: mathewc <1056548+mathewc@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Skills now match workflows by category, tags, and name keywords

Root cause: analyzeWorkflow didn't pass workflow category/tags to
selectRelevantSkills, so custom skills with matching category (e.g.,
'compliance' skill for 'compliance-posture' workflow) were never selected.

Fixes:
- analyzeWorkflow accepts workflowMeta param (category, tags, workflowId)
- selectRelevantSkills now receives category + tags + workflow name keywords
- Workflow name is split into keywords for tag matching (e.g., 'HIPAA
  Compliance Check' → matches skill with tag 'HIPAA')
- API route passes workflowCategory, workflowTags, workflowId from client
- ExecutionPanel accepts workflowMeta, passes to analyze API call
- WorkflowsContent looks up workflow category/tags from catalog list

Example: HIPAA skill (category: compliance, tags: [HIPAA]) now matches
HIPAA workflow (category: compliance-posture) via category map +
workflow name keyword 'hipaa' matching skill tag 'HIPAA'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: refresh app token before benchmark push (#27)

* fix(ci): refresh app token before benchmark push

The GitHub App token generated at checkout expires after 1 hour.
The full benchmark suite takes longer than that, so by the time the
push step runs the token is stale.

Move token generation to right before the push step so it gets a
fresh token, and set the remote URL explicitly with it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(benchmarks): allow better-sqlite3 native build in pnpm

better-sqlite3 was missing from onlyBuiltDependencies in
pnpm-workspace.yaml, so pnpm silently skipped compiling its native
addon during install. This caused all Node.js benchmark apps using
the db scenario to be skipped with a 'Could not locate the bindings
file' error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Allow all users to create and manage API keys (#215)

* Allow all users to create and manage API keys

Move API key management from admin-only to user settings. All authenticated users can now create, view, and revoke their own API keys. Backend routes moved from /admin/api-keys to /me/api-keys, UI moved from dedicated admin page to Settings. Admin checks removed since the service layer already scopes keys per creator.

* Extract API keys UI into ApiKeysSection component

Address review feedback: extract the API key management UI (table, create/revoke/display modals) from Settings.svelte into a dedicated ApiKeysSection component. Also suppress 401 error toasts during loadApiKeys to avoid flashing errors when unauthenticated users hit /settings before the redirect to /login fires.

* Update frontend/src/routes.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Patrick Veverka <veverkap@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename admin_api_key.go to api_key_handler.go

The handler file is no longer admin-specific since routes moved to /me. Update the filename and docs reference to match.

* Remove invalid string redirect for /admin/api-keys route

The "/admin/api-keys": "/settings" entry used a string value instead of a
Svelte component, which broke svelte-spa-router entirely and caused all
pages to fail to render. Simply remove the route since the admin API keys
page no longer exists.

* Update screenshot script for API keys in Settings

Remove the admin/api-keys entry from the admin pages list since that page
no longer exists. Add screenshots of the API Keys section within the
Settings page instead.

* Update docs/architecture.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Patrick Veverka <veverkap@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add PR comment notifications with deduplication

Created a new notification channel that posts comments directly on PRs
about conflicts, with built-in duplicate detection.

Features:
- ENABLE_PR_COMMENTS env var to opt-in (default: false)
- Posts comment on both PRs in a conflict pair
- Checks for existing comments before posting (signature + PR number)
- Integrates with deduplication (only comments on new/changed conflicts)
- Respects DRY_RUN mode (logs what would be posted)
- Comment includes: conflicting files, line ranges, other PR details

Comment format:
- Clear warning header with bot signature
- Link to conflicting PR with title
- List of files with overlapping line ranges
- Actionable guidance for developers
- Attribution to pr-conflict-detector

Duplicate prevention:
- Before posting, checks if comment already exists for this conflict pair
- If signature + other PR number found in existing comments, skips posting
- Error-safe: if check fails, assumes no comment to avoid blocking

Test coverage: 190 tests passing, 99% overall coverage, 9.92/10 pylint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
